title: Marine Le Pen to find out whether she can run for president
subhead: A court will rule on Tuesday in Marine Le Pen's appeal trial over alleged misappropriation of European Union funds. Its decision will determine whether she can run in the 2027 presidential election.

US leads international concern after China test-fires missile into Pacific
subhead: Though a spokesperson for China's navy stated on Monday that the test launch was simply a 'routine arrangement' of military training, the US state department is concerned that this demonstrates China's growing capacity to strike the US mainland.

title: 'A despicable woman unworthy of her office': Kylian Mbappé responds to racist remarks from Paraguayan senator
subhead: The captain of the French national football team responded on Monday to a racist message posted by Celeste Amarilla. The Paraguayan politician's comments came in the wake of the Albirroja's defeat by Les Bleus in the round of 16 of the World Cup.

title: 2026 World Cup: Spain beat Portugal 1-0 to reach quarter-finals
subhead: The European champions will face the United States, the only one of the co-hosts remaining, or Belgium on Friday in Los Angeles.

title: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
subhead: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

subhead: In her final public appearance before the ruling on Tuesday that will determine whether she can run for president, Marine Le Pen focused on highlighting her partnership with Jordan Bardella.

title: Vladimir Putin, or the loneliness of 'Homo Sovieticus'
subhead: Between denial of reality, a desire for revenge and carefully crafted messaging, the Russian president has tried to position himself as the master of the game. But after more than four years of war in Ukraine, these old tricks inherited from the Soviet past are showing their limitations, and he is losing credibility.

title: 250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subhead: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.
